contracted from H1 (אָב) and H3498 (יָתַר); father of abundance (i.e. liberal);

18.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
18.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

18.gl: to exist, i.e. be or become, come to pass (always emphatic, and not a mere copula or auxiliary)
18.kj: beacon, [idiom] altogether, be(-come), accomplished, committed, like), break, cause, come (to pass), do, faint, fall, [phrase] follow, happen, [idiom] have, last, pertain, quit (on

18.gl: literally one officiating, a priest; also (by courtesy) an acting priest (although a layman)
18.kj: chief ruler, [idiom] own, priest, prince, principal officer.

18.dv: probably from H3772 (כָּרַת) in the sense of executioner; (compare H2876 (טַבָּח)) (only collectively in the singular as plural)

18.gl: Jehojada, the name of three Israelites
Benajah, the name of twelve Israelites
18.dv: or בּנָיָהוּ; (prolonged) from H1129 (בָּנָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has built;
